mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL indicates accelerating inflation, prompting expectations of tighter monetary policy which increases discount rates and negatively impacts equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive 20d returns occur in >65% of rising inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack reduces wage pressure and signals economic deceleration, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in RSAFS and HOUST signal a broad-based contraction in consumer and housing demand, reducing near-term revenue growth prospects for S&P 500 companies and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening where FEDFUNDS rises above 4.0%, higher borrowing costs compress profit margins and equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or positive returns dominate in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding INDPRO reflects strengthening industrial output and economic momentum, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below -0.02 over rolling windows, or rising INDPRO correlates with negative 5d returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in PAYEMS exceeding 200 thousand in a single month indicates an overheating labor market that precedes Fed tightening, triggering negative equity returns over the next month as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20d returns remain positive in >60% of months following PAYEMS surges above 200k, invalidating the overheating channel.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth decelerates while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, stagflationary pressures erode real consumer purchasing power and corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combination of slowing GDPC1 and high CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 60d returns, or positive returns exceed 55% in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, rising INDPRO combined with stable FEDFUNDS signals resilient economic activity without immediate rate hike threats, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns do not materialize in >50% of recovery periods with stable rates, or the signal loses predictive power in walk-forward tests.
